agent_notes: Merton is a quality-over-volume borough for PAINTS: Morden Hall Park, Wimbledon Village and Merton Abbey Mills are strong anchors, but true bookshop-cafe and current-exhibition supply is thin.
Creative Walks are limited to Morden Hall Park because Mitcham Common is explicitly removed from the PAINTS v6 safety list and Wimbledon Common is not on the approved list.
Several venues have public opening hours but not public group walk-in guarantees; these are marked UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.

venues.venue_name: Morden Hall Park Wandle & Snuff Mills Walk
venues.opening_hours: Park/open-access hours not fully stated on public source; National Trust visitor facilities and cafe hours vary. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm before publishing.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access park route; no booking needed for the walk itself. Meeting/weather backup cafe walk-in status UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 in daytime: broad paths, open meadows, Wandle bridges and cafe/toilet infrastructure.
venues.capacity_notes: Route can split naturally into pairs at pinch points near bridges; avoid dusk and flooded paths.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday or Sunday 10:30-12:30, finishing before peak cafe queues.
venues.why_fits_paints: A rare outer-London walk that feels genuinely designed rather than incidental: watermeadows, old mill buildings, bridges, rose garden and National Trust stewardship. It gives a host real story material without sending strangers into a remote-feeling route. PAINTS v6 names Morden Hall Park as a Premium Launch Park.
venues.anecdote_story: The estate sits on the River Wandle and includes former snuff mill buildings, stableyard, leats and bridges; the National Trust notes events, exhibitions and a second-hand bookshop/cafe ecosystem around the park.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Suggested stops: Potting Shed Cafe start; Wandle bridge/leat; Snuff Mill/stableyard; rose garden or wetland edge; optional Deen City Farm edge only in daylight.
venues.safety_notes: PAINTS-approved Premium Launch Park. Daytime only recommended; host should avoid secluded paths after dark and check weather/flooding.
venues.backup_venue: The Potting Shed Cafe, Morden Hall Park
venues.risks_watchouts: Cafe seating for 8-12 and exact seasonal hours are not guaranteed publicly; use as weather fallback only after host check.

venues.venue_name: Merton Art Society Summer Exhibition at Morden Hall Park Stableyard
venues.opening_hours: Exhibition listed 27 Jun-12 Jul 2026 / 4 Jul-12 Jul 2026, 10:00-17:00; venue facility hours UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: National Trust event page says booking not needed and free event; group of 8-12 still UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely suitable as a slow-look gallery visit with cafe/walk spillover; avoid arriving as one dense group if the room is small.
venues.capacity_notes: Stableyard Exhibition Space size not publicly quantified.
venues.best_time_slot: Weekend 10:30-12:00 or weekday 11:00-12:30 during exhibition dates.
venues.why_fits_paints: Local artists, works for sale and a National Trust stableyard setting make this a stronger PAINTS fit than a generic library-room show. The exhibition gives people something tangible to discuss, then the park gives the event a decompression route. It also balances Wimbledon-heavy picks with Morden.
venues.anecdote_story: Merton Art Society is presented as a local group with over 60 years of activity; the 2026 show includes more than 60 original works across paintings, miniatures and art cards.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Over 60 original artworks by Merton Art Society members, with many works for sale.
venues.safety_notes: Located inside PAINTS-approved Morden Hall Park; daytime arrival/departure recommended.
venues.backup_venue: Morden Hall Park Wandle & Snuff Mills Walk
venues.risks_watchouts: Current-exhibition source is official/community rather than major press; date window is short.

venues.venue_name: Her Court at Wimbledon Museum / Norman Plastow Gallery
venues.opening_hours: Exhibition listed daily 11:00-17:00 from 2-11 Jul 2026; museum normal opening Fri-Sun 14:30-17:00. Host must confirm exhibition access on chosen date.
venues.walk_in_policy: Exhibition appears open daily/free; timed-entry not indicated. Group walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good if staggered through the gallery, then reconvene outside or at a nearby cafe.
venues.why_fits_paints: This has the clearest current-exhibition hook in Wimbledon: contemporary art connected to local suffragette history. It brings identity, place and visual work into one compact event. The museum's local-history base gives a host more to work with than a standard white-wall show.
venues.anecdote_story: The show explores the role Wimbledon played in the suffragette movement through a Young Masters exhibition project in collaboration with Wimbledon Museum.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Contemporary art on women's stories, achievement, identity and cultural influence, tied to Wimbledon suffragette history.
venues.safety_notes: Village daytime setting; use Wimbledon station or bus connections and avoid routing through unapproved commons as part of the official event.
venues.risks_watchouts: Very short exhibition run; host must confirm whether a group of 8-12 can enter together.

venues.opening_hours: Every Saturday 09:00-13:00; rain or shine per Eventbrite/Facebook. Stall attendance varies.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; meeting point cafe walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for browsing in pairs and regrouping outside the school gate; not ideal for standing as one group at stalls.
venues.capacity_notes: School-ground market; no public seating guarantee.
venues.why_fits_paints: Producer-led, weekly and established, with enough sensory material for a market-day conversation. It is more grounded than glossy, which suits PAINTS if hosted as a slow browse with prompts. The 25-year signal gives it a community anchor.
venues.anecdote_story: London Farmers' Markets lists the market at Wimbledon Park Primary; a 2025 post marked 25 years of Wimbledon Farmers Market.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Farm produce, seasonal flowers, baked goods and ready-to-eat food; stall mix varies weekly.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime school-site market; host should set a clear meet point outside the entrance.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx 15 min by bus/taxi or longer walk; within market backup transit allowance
venues.risks_watchouts: Dogs restricted at school entrance; no public loos listed; stall mix changes weekly.

venues.opening_hours: Every Sunday 10:00-15:00 per Wimbledon Village and Facebook/Instagram signals; older third-party pages list 10:00-14:00, so host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a Sunday browse if participants split into smaller shopping clusters.
venues.capacity_notes: Outdoor High Street market; seating depends on nearby cafes/pubs.
venues.why_fits_paints: The village setting gives a softer, more scenic market version than central Wimbledon. The farm produce and ready-to-eat offer meet the Market Days rule without needing a mall or chain anchor. It pairs well with Wimbledon Books or the museum.
venues.anecdote_story: Wimbledon Village promotes the market as a weekly local farmers' market with rotating stalls and farm-fresh/local products.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Farm produce, bread, cakes, fish, meat, cheese, olives, juices and coffee depending on weekly traders.
venues.safety_notes: Busy village daytime setting; do not route the official event through Wimbledon Common because it is not on the PAINTS approved park list.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx 15 min by bus/taxi or longer walk; within market backup transit allowance
venues.risks_watchouts: Hours conflict across sources; confirm current operator schedule before publishing.

venues.opening_hours: Every 2nd and last Saturday of the month, 09:00-15:00 per Merton Abbey Mills; Instagram references 10:00 onwards. Host must confirm exact date/time.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong: riverside site, independent shops and food/drink allow pairs to browse and regroup.
venues.capacity_notes: Outdoor/indoor mix; weather resilience better than a bare car-park market.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is Merton's most PAINTS-coded market: craft, food, watermill setting and William Morris/Liberty textile history. It rewards the slight inconvenience of Colliers Wood with a real place-story. The presence of Only Make Believe Books strengthens the cultural texture.
venues.anecdote_story: Merton Abbey Mills sits on former textile/printing works associated with William Morris and Liberty; Merton Abbey Mills describes the market as an award-winning event with food, drink and crafts.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Market stalls, food, drink, independent shops, bookshop and riverside mill setting.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime recommended; watch narrow riverside edges and set a clear meet point near the watermill/market entrance.
venues.backup_venue: Only Make Believe Books and Merton Abbey Mills shops
venues.risks_watchouts: Market only runs on selected Saturdays; stall quality may vary.

venues.opening_hours: First Thursday evening monthly; July 2026 Instagram post advertised Thu 2 Jul, 18:30-22:00. Future dates UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market unless specific events change; host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good if treated as an early-evening food/craft browse; less robust for late departures.
venues.capacity_notes: Outdoor/evening crowding and vendor availability unknown.
venues.best_time_slot: First Thursday 18:30-20:30, with host-managed station departures.
venues.why_fits_paints: The night market gives Merton Abbey Mills a more social PAINTS format: food, drink, crafts and live music against the mill backdrop. It is more atmospheric than the daytime market, but evening logistics are less robust. Tier B because hours/date cadence and group seating need confirmation.
venues.anecdote_story: Merton Abbey Mills links its modern independent-market identity to the historic William Morris and Liberty printworks site.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Street food, drinks, crafts and live music.
venues.safety_notes: Evening event; host should lead the group to Colliers Wood together and avoid lingering on quieter riverside paths after dark.
venues.risks_watchouts: Weather, monthly schedule, vendor lineup and late-evening transit safety require host confirmation.

venues.opening_hours: Council/social posts reference monthly Saturday markets; recent post says 4th Saturday monthly 09:00-14:00 at Mickleham North Community Centre. Host must confirm exact active site and hours.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Possible for a community-market browse; less polished than Wimbledon/Merton Abbey Mills.
venues.capacity_notes: Stall quantity and seating not guaranteed.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is included for geographic counterweight rather than glamour. It may work for a host who wants a grounded, local-maker/community story in Mitcham. Tier B because the public sources are inconsistent on date, location and cadence.
venues.anecdote_story: Merton Council has promoted Mitcham Market as a craft and food market featuring local businesses and free activities.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Local craft, food and community stalls when active.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime only; host should confirm exact meeting point and route to station.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx 8-10 min from Mitcham centre depending on market site
venues.risks_watchouts: Unclear current cadence/site; do not publish without council/operator confirmation.

venues.opening_hours: Thursday-Friday 10:00-17:00, Saturday-Sunday 09:00-17:00 per official site/Merton Abbey Mills tenant page.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookshop browsing appears walk-in; seating/coffee and group tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Best as a browsing-led Books & Coffee event with pairs rotating, not as 12 people seated for two hours inside the shop.
venues.capacity_notes: Described as tiny/teeny tiny; host should cap dwell time inside and use nearby cafe/market seating.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday or Sunday 09:30-11:00 before market/cafe peak.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the strongest Books & Coffee candidate in Merton because it is genuinely book-led and embedded in a characterful mill site. It has fiction focus, social bookshop language and nearby food/drink texture. Operationally it is small, so the format must be carefully hosted.
venues.anecdote_story: The shop describes itself as a teeny tiny fiction-led bookshop in Merton Abbey Mills; social posts frame it as a magical bookshop beside an old water mill.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Contemporary and classic fiction for adults and children; rare/vintage signals appear in community posts; nearby cafes/market for coffee.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime Merton Abbey Mills setting; keep group movement together between Colliers Wood and the Mills.
venues.backup_venue: Abbey Mills Market or Potting Shed-style nearby cafe at Merton Abbey Mills
venues.risks_watchouts: Not a conventional full cafe; two-hour seated reading format is not verified.

venues.venue_name: Wimbledon Books with Village Coffee Pairing
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookshop walk-in appears accepted; partner cafe/group seating UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable as a browse-and-discuss format with nearby cafe/pub spillover; not verified as a two-hour seated bookshop event.
venues.capacity_notes: No on-site cafe found; needs literal nearby coffee partner confirmation.
venues.best_time_slot: Sunday 11:00-12:30, paired with village market or quiet cafe.
venues.why_fits_paints: A long-running independent bookshop in the village gives Merton a legitimate literary anchor. It does not fully satisfy the raised Books & Coffee rule on its own because the coffee is nearby rather than clearly on-site/next-door. Tier B, useful only with a confirmed independent cafe pairing.
venues.anecdote_story: Bookshop Crawl lists Wimbledon Books as an independent bookshop founded in 1996, stocking adult fiction/non-fiction, local history, children's books, gifts and stationery.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Independent general bookshop stock; local history and children's sections.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime village setting; do not route through Wimbledon Common as official walk component.
venues.risks_watchouts: Coffee component is the weak link; host must confirm an independent cafe that can tolerate 8-12.

venues.opening_hours: Mon-Wed 15:00-23:00; Thu-Fri 15:00-23:30; Sat-Sun 12:00-23:30; Bank Holidays 12:00-23:00 per Hop Back/CAMRA.
venues.walk_in_policy: Pub walk-ins expected; group of 8-12 tolerance UNVERIFIED - host should call ahead.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good if arriving early and using larger tables/conservatory; call ahead for a single table.
venues.capacity_notes: CAMRA notes large tables, two bars and conservatory.
venues.best_time_slot: Thursday 17:30-19:30 or Sunday 15:00-17:00; avoid AFC Wimbledon peaks.
venues.why_fits_paints: The Sultan is the rare Merton drinks pick with both backstreet character and a defensible story. It is Hop Back Brewery's only London pub, with CAMRA validation and conversation-friendly large tables. It is beer-led rather than natural-wine-led, but strongly fits the character pub branch of the category.
venues.anecdote_story: CAMRA/London Beer Guide describe a 1950s brick pub replacing a wartime-demolished original, rescued from likely permanent closure in 1994 by Hop Back Brewery and now its only London pub.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Hop Back cask beers, cider, wine and pub drinks; beer festivals/music festival signals.
venues.safety_notes: Backstreet residential setting; host should route attendees together to South Wimbledon or Colliers Wood after dark.
venues.risks_watchouts: Beer-led rather than wine-led; football/event days may crowd the pub.

venues.opening_hours: Official site: Mon-Fri 16:00-23:00; Sat 12:00-00:00; Sun 12:00-22:00. CAMRA notes Bank Holiday Monday 12:00-22:30.
venues.walk_in_policy: Pub walk-ins likely, but group of 8-12 and table availability UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Potentially good via beer garden/conservatory, especially off-peak.
venues.capacity_notes: CAMRA notes large public area and conservatory; CityStack highlights beer garden.
venues.why_fits_paints: A colourful, independent local pub with garden texture makes this a workable backup drinks option. It has visual warmth and more personality than a chain bar. Tier B because rating/category fit is weaker than The Sultan and exact group walk-in policy is unverified.
venues.anecdote_story: CityStack/Instagram describe it as Wimbledon's oldest surviving independent pub; the official site lists WWT Garden Shed Ltd.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Pub drinks, pizzas/burgers/sharing food, beer garden.
venues.safety_notes: Use Haydons Road or South Wimbledon routes; after dark, host should keep departures paired.
venues.risks_watchouts: Sports/private-hire energy may be louder than ideal; public rating evidence is mixed.

venues.opening_hours: Social profile states Mon-Thu 16:00-23:00, Fri-Sun 12:00-23:00; host must confirm because official website was not found.
venues.walk_in_policy: Freehouse/pub walk-ins likely; group of 8-12 UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Possible only off-peak; described historically as thin/small.
venues.capacity_notes: Small pub; not a robust first choice for 12 unless host checks ahead.
venues.best_time_slot: Sunday jazz/afternoon or weekday early evening after confirmation.
venues.why_fits_paints: The Trafalgar has the scruffy local-story energy PAINTS often likes, including a community save/reopening narrative. It is not polished and operational details are thin, so it sits as Tier B rather than a launch-perfect pick. Use when the host wants old-Wimbledon freehouse character.
venues.anecdote_story: Community sources and Save the Traf note a local campaign around the pub; social profile describes it as Wimbledon's oldest freehouse and says it reopened in 2023 after several years closed.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Real ales, ciders and craft beers; older Time Out listing references Sunday jazz.
venues.safety_notes: High Path/South Wimbledon evening route should be host-led; do not rely on late solo departures.
venues.risks_watchouts: Hours, rating floor and group capacity need direct confirmation; if unconfirmed, do not publish.

rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Explicitly removed in the PAINTS v6 safety list; cannot be sourced or used as a backup even though it is locally obvious.
Not on PAINTS Premium Launch or Safety-First park lists, so it must be flagged for team review and not sourced as a Creative Walk.
Potentially attractive heritage grounds, but not on the PAINTS-approved Creative Walk safety list; current events could work separately only after team review.
Strong community story and good ratings, but it is a Young's pub; PAINTS brief rejects chain/templated venues for Tier A and Merton has better independent/backstreet options.
Historic and attractive, but Young's-operated and directly adjacent to unapproved Wimbledon Common; not a clean PAINTS drinks pick under this brief.

Excellent location at Merton Abbey Mills and usable as a backup, but public review signal found was below/unclear against PAINTS rating floor and it reads more general family pub than Show & Tell Drinks.
Appealing Wandle/textile heritage and good backup for Mitcham, but no current notable exhibition with recent press found; opening hours are also narrow and volunteer-dependent.
Operationally strong museum, but the PAINTS category requires paintings, sculpture, textiles, ceramics or craft and a current exhibition that justifies the event; this is primarily sports heritage.
Council-run street market with useful local function, but the public source does not show enough curated maker/food identity for PAINTS Market Days.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: General independent coffee shops in Wimbledon/Colliers Wood
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Rejected unless tied to a substantive book offer; PAINTS v6 says pure specialty coffee with no serious book component is not qualifying.
